name: hephaestus (disputed)
shortDescription: The Greek god of blacksmiths, metalworking, fire, and crafts. His parentage is debated, with some accounts claiming Hera alone bore him.
description: Hephaestus, in Greek mythology, is the god of blacksmiths, metalworking, carpenters, craftsmen, artisans, sculptors, metallurgy, fire, and volcanoes. He is often depicted as lame or deformed, a result of being thrown from Mount Olympus, either by Hera, his mother, ashamed of his appearance, or by Zeus, for siding with Hera in a quarrel. Despite his physical impairment, Hephaestus was a skilled artisan, creating magnificent weapons, armor, and other objects for the gods and heroes. He is the smith of the gods and is associated with the forge. He crafted Achilles' armor, Hermes' winged helmet and sandals, Aphrodite's girdle, and Agamemnon's scepter. His symbols include the hammer, anvil, and tongs. He is married to Aphrodite, though she is often unfaithful. The dispute surrounding his parentage stems from varying accounts; while traditionally considered the son of Zeus and Hera, some versions of the myth claim Hera conceived him alone, out of spite for Zeus' solo creation of Athena. This alternative origin underscores Hera's power and independence. Hephaestus's creations played a vital role in many myths, showcasing his importance to both gods and mortals.
